instruction:
Find the integral:
$$
\int \frac{ 1 }{ \sqrt[3]{\left(\sin(x)\right)^{11} \cdot \cos(x)} } \, dx
$$

Options:
A. -\frac{3\cdot\left(1+4\cdot\left(\tan(x)\right)^2\right)}{8\cdot\left(\tan(x)\right)^2\cdot\sqrt[3]{\left(\tan(x)\right)^5}}+C
B. -\frac{3\cdot\left(1+4\cdot\left(\tan(x)\right)^2\right)}{8\cdot\left(\tan(x)\right)^2\cdot\sqrt[3]{\left(\tan(x)\right)^3}}+C
C. -\frac{3\cdot\left(1+4\cdot\left(\tan(x)\right)^2\right)}{8\cdot\left(\tan(x)\right)^2\cdot\sqrt[3]{\left(\tan(x)\right)^8}}+C
D. -\frac{3\cdot\left(1+4\cdot\left(\tan(x)\right)^2\right)}{8\cdot\left(\tan(x)\right)^2\cdot\sqrt[3]{\left(\tan(x)\right)^6}}+C
E. -\frac{3\cdot\left(1+4\cdot\left(\tan(x)\right)^2\right)}{8\cdot\left(\tan(x)\right)^2\cdot\sqrt[3]{\left(\tan(x)\right)^2}}+C
F. -\frac{3\cdot\left(1+4\cdot\left(\tan(x)\right)^2\right)}{8\cdot\left(\tan(x)\right)^3\cdot\sqrt[3]{\left(\tan(x)\right)^2}}+C
G. -\frac{3\cdot\left(1+4\cdot\left(\tan(x)\right)^2\right)}{8\cdot\left(\tan(x)\right)^2\cdot\sqrt[3]{\left(\tan(x)\right)^4}}+C
H. -\frac{3\cdot\left(1+4\cdot\left(\tan(x)\right)^2\right)}{8\cdot\left(\tan(x)\right)^2\cdot\sqrt[3]{\left(\tan(x)\right)^1}}+C
I. -\frac{3\cdot\left(1+4\cdot\left(\tan(x)\right)^2\right)}{8\cdot\left(\tan(x)\right)^4\cdot\sqrt[3]{\left(\tan(x)\right)^2}}+C
J. -\frac{3\cdot\left(1+4\cdot\left(\tan(x)\right)^2\right)}{8\cdot\left(\tan(x)\right)^2\cdot\sqrt[3]{\left(\tan(x)\right)^7}}+C
